title: cotyledon umbilicus-veneris var. patula
intro: Cotyledon umbilicus-veneris var. patula, commonly known as the Venus's whip or variegated St. John's wort, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Cotyledon umbilicus-veneris var. patula include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide protection against infection. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
